title: Claude Fable 5
why_matters: Anthropic's most capable general-release model yet launches at less than half the price of its predecessor, lowering the cost floor for deploying frontier AI.

title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
why_matters: The world's most valuable consumer electronics company has effectively outsourced its AI foundation layer to Google — a structural shift in how Big Tech acquires AI capability.

title: German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
why_matters: The first ruling to classify AI-generated search summaries as the publisher's own speech — not a link — establishing a liability precedent with wide implications for every AI product that surfaces factual claims.

title: A giant star may have destroyed itself in one of the universe's rarest explosions
why_matters: Headline only — significance cannot be assessed without the article text.

title: FCC wants to kill burner phones by forcing telecoms to get all customers' IDs
why_matters: A near-anonymous phone plan is currently a practical safety tool for abuse survivors, whistleblowers, and journalists; this rule would eliminate it in the US.

title: Microsoft open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
why_matters: Supply-chain malware in tools used by AI developers puts credentials for cloud systems and customer data at risk; the re-compromise pattern suggests the initial response may not have fully removed the attackers.

title: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
why_matters: These defaults close the main code-execution paths that supply-chain attackers exploit during npm install; projects that have not audited their dependency scripts will break on upgrade.

title: What it feels like to work with Mythos
why_matters: An independent researcher's hands-on account confirms the step-change claims Anthropic made at launch and gives a concrete picture of how the model behaves on sustained, complex tasks.

title: Let's Encrypt bans certificate usage in any US sanctioned territory
why_matters: Headline only — significance cannot be assessed without the full agreement text.

title: Cleaning up after AI rockstar developers
why_matters: As AI-assisted development becomes the norm, the technical debt patterns it creates are becoming visible; this is an early map of what that debt looks like and how to avoid it.

title: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
why_matters: These defaults close the main code-execution paths that supply-chain attackers exploit during npm install; projects that auto-run scripts will break on upgrade.

title: Microsoft open source tools hacked to steal AI developers' passwords
why_matters: Supply-chain malware in tools used by AI developers puts credentials for cloud systems and customer data at risk, and the breadth of affected projects suggests systematic targeting.

title: Waymo builds a better benchmark for comparing robotaxis to humans
why_matters: A credible behavioural benchmark lets Waymo and regulators make more honest comparisons between robotaxi safety and human driving, which is the central argument for commercial deployment.

title: Claude Fable 5 (Mythos 5) — Anthropic launches its most capable public model
why_matters: Anthropic is releasing a measurably more capable model while publicly explaining the capability-safety trade-off: intentionally limited in some areas to ship without major misuse risk.

title: German court rules Google liable for false AI Overview answers
why_matters: A German court ruling that AI-generated summaries are the publisher's own words establishes a liability framework where search providers bear direct responsibility for AI inaccuracies.

title: Avalanche's desktop fusion reactor hits 11 million degrees — a key milestone
why_matters: Reaching 1 keV at desktop scale and below $50M in spend suggests the cost curve for fusion research is compressing faster than the industry assumed.

title: Claude Fable 5 — Anthropic's most capable public model, with conservative safety guardrails
why_matters: The model represents the largest publicly available capability jump Anthropic has released, and the two-tier release (Fable for general use, Mythos for vetted defenders) sets a new template for how frontier models are deployed.

title: David Sinclair to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in humans via $101M XPrize
why_matters: The first human trial of an oral epigenetic reprogramming compound, backed by a $101M incentive prize, moves longevity from theoretical biology into a competitive clinical race with a concrete measurable target.

title: 'AI-pilled' firms are spending $7,500 per employee per month on AI — and growing
why_matters: The 14% month-on-month growth in AI spend among power users signals that AI budgets are on a steep adoption curve, not a plateau — and the multi-model hedging strategy shows enterprises are avoiding lock-in.

title: India pauses Starlink rollout before SpaceX IPO over Iran compliance breach
why_matters: A compliance breach in one country has become a pre-IPO liability for a $200B+ company: it exposes how sovereign access, not technology, is the binding constraint on satellite internet economics.
